03 — Screenplay Synopsis

Tone

A disorienting look through the performance of death and the horrors of coincidence.

Status

Est. Runtime

03

Last Stop

[ Your one-sentence logline. ]

Opening paragraph:

Ext. James truck - NIGHT
A faded red pickup truck drives past on a gravel road.
After driving through miles of unlit forest road it moves to the shoulder, stopping abruptly a couple hundred feet from a railroad crossing.
JAMES, 20's, disheveled, blood-stained clothes, barefooted, average build, sits in the truck for a moment looking out the windshield. He then violently opens the door, jumping out barefooted onto the sharp gravel.
James
(In pain)
God damnit those fucking boots!
He slams the car door and gingerly walks to the grass on the side of the road. He's holding his left side like his rib is broken.

 Inciting incident:

Protagonist witnesses a murder which puts him on the wrong side of a sociopathic serial killer. 

Act 2: 

Protagonist is framed by villain for one of the murders that he committed

Resolution:

Serial killer ends up being tried and found guilty. Last scene is two important supporting characters parting ways knowing that one will have to kill the other, a gun shot goes off as the camera pans to the sky, unsure of who just died.
